items.title: investingLive European markets wrap: Setting up for that TACO moment? SpaceX debut up next
items.summary: Headlines: Iran will not restore Strait of Hormuz status to pre-war level - IRNA The US-Iran MoU could be signed as soon as Sunday in Geneva - BBG Here is what the US-Iran deal looks set to be like after another TACO moment Iran media says that memorandum of understanding draft still not yet finalised Oil prices plunge after Iran confirms details of the deal with the US The countdown continues ahead of the Wall Street open, all eyes on SpaceX ECB policymaker Nagel says keeping all options open for July meeting ECB's Dolenc says current rate level gives enough flexibility to respond to energy shock Core inflation confirmed to have picked up in Germany during May French inflation accelerates in May as services inflation push up Spanish inflation picks up in May, core prices also continue to nudge higher UK economy sees marginal contraction in April as services sector cools Markets: WTI crude down 3.5% to $84.60 US dollar little changed, keeps steadier Equities stay optimistic, European indices trade over 1% higher S&P 500 futures up 0.5%, Nasdaq futures up 0.5% with eyes on SpaceX debut US 10-year yields down 0.2 bps to 4.46% Gold down 0.2% to $4,204 Bitcoin up 0.4% to $63,631 The main story of the session was that Iran has emerged with key details of its deal with the US. And it is either a case of there going to be another major TACO moment or this deal is going to fall apart before it even begins. The details point to many concessions being offered up by the US with the summarised version here . And if so, it is essentially a reset back to 27 February. Tell me, what was the point of this war again? As the details surfaced, markets are cheering on the headlines but not taking things all too far. As a reminder, there will still be another 60 days of negotiations after this. And besides the point, we're still yet to see how Trump can spin this all into a "win". Oil prices are down with WTI crude lower by 3.5% to $84.60. The low earlier touched $83.20 before Iran came out to say that they will still be the ones in charge over the Strait of Hormuz pending any final agreement. Meanwhile, the dollar is keeping steadier after a slight nudge lower on the headlines. EUR/USD is flat again at 1.1575 while USD/JPY sits higher by 0.1% at 160.15 on the day. Besides that, AUD/USD is down 0.1% to 0.7040 on the day. In the equities space, European indices are posting solid gains in looking to wrap up the week. The DAX is up 1.5% with the CAC 40 up 1.7% currently. As for US futures, the mood music remains bright with all eyes on the SpaceX trading debut later today. S&P 500 futures are up 0.5% with Nasdaq futures also up 0.5%. As for precious metals, gold raced higher on the US-Iran news early on but saw gains tempered after. The high touched $4,245 before price is dropping back lower to be down 0.3% on the day at $4,200. Deal or no deal? We can only wait and see. But whatever the case is, just be reminded that it doesn't mean that this is "the end" of the war. For some context, this is how things may unfold with regards to the Strait of Hormuz: This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 头条新闻：伊朗不会将霍尔木兹海峡状况恢复至战前水平——伊朗通讯社（IRNA） 美伊谅解备忘录最早可能于周日在日内瓦签署——彭博社 经历又一个“TACO时刻”后，美伊协议预计将呈现如下局面 伊朗媒体称谅解备忘录草案尚未最终敲定 伊朗确认与美国协议细节后，油价暴跌 华尔街开盘前倒计时仍在继续， 万众瞩目SpaceX欧洲央行决策者纳格尔表示7月会议将保持所有选项开放 欧洲央行多伦茨称当前利率水平为应对能源冲击提供了足够灵活性 德国5月核心通胀率确认回升 法国5月通胀加速，服务业通胀推高 西班牙5月通胀回升，核心物价也持续小幅走高 英国4月经济微幅萎缩，服务业降温 市场： WTI原油下跌3.5%至84.60美元 美元汇率基本持平，走势趋稳 股市保持乐观，欧洲股指上涨逾1% 标普500指数期货上涨0.5%， 纳斯达克期货上涨0.5%，市场关注SpaceX首次公开募股 美国10年期国债收益率下跌0.2个基点至4.46% 黄金下跌0.2%至4,204美元 比特币上涨0.4%至63,631美元 该 本轮会谈的焦点在于，伊朗披露了其与美国达成协议的关键细节。这要么预示着又一个重大的“TACO时刻”即将到来，要么意味着这项协议甚至在正式启动前就会破裂。这些细节表明美国做出了诸多让步，摘要版本见此处。如果真是如此，这实质上就是将谈判重置回2月27日的状态。请问，这场战争的意义究竟何在？随着细节浮出水面，市场对相关头条新闻反应积极，但并未过度乐观。需要提醒的是，此后还将有60天的谈判期。更关键的是，我们尚未看到特朗普将如何将这一切包装成一场“胜利”。油价出现下跌，西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）下跌3.5%，报84.60美元。早盘低点曾触及83.20美元，随后伊朗方面表示，在最终协议达成之前，霍尔木兹海峡的控制权仍将掌握在他们手中。与此同时，美元在受相关消息影响小幅走低后，走势趋于平稳。欧元兑美元维持在1.1575不变，而美元兑日元当日上涨0.1%，报160.15。此外，澳元兑美元当日下跌0.1%，报0.7040。 在股市方面，欧洲股指本周收官之际表现强劲。目前，DAX指数上涨1.5%，CAC 40指数上涨1.7%。至于美国股指期货，市场情绪依然乐观，所有目光都聚焦于今天晚些时候SpaceX的首次公开交易。标普500指数期货上涨0.5%，纳斯达克指数期货同样上涨0.5%。贵金属方面，受美伊局势消息影响，黄金早盘一度飙升，但随后涨幅有所收窄。金价盘中一度触及4,245美元，随后回落，目前报4,200美元，日跌幅0.3%。能达成协议吗？我们只能拭目以待。但无论结果如何，请谨记这并不意味着这场战争的“终结”。为便于理解，以下是霍尔木兹海峡局势可能的发展路径：本文由investinglive.com的Justin Low撰写。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Equites stage solid rallies on Iran deal optimism
items.summary: Reuters poll in near unanimous: RBA seen holding at 4.35% on June 16 Westpac sees RBA hold at the June 15-16 meeting, but more hikes ahead IRGC vows "regret-inducing" response, signals no surrender on deal Report - US forces down Iranian drones going for strikes on Hormuz shipping PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8109 (vs. estimate at 6.7640) South Korea’s central bank governor Shin says interest rates need to be increased Japan's Nikkei share index up more than 4% on Trump-Iran 'deal' CME to launch 24/7 trading for micro oil and gold contracts. 24/7 futures. US, Canada delay Gordie Howe bridge opening after Trump objects More on this - Iran blocks Hormuz tanker as closure threat takes effect Reports: new Supreme Leader yet to bless Trump's Iran deal NZ manufacturing PMI dips to 49.9 as Mideast conflict bites. Fuel costs, demand weigh. Hormuz tanker blocked as Iran disputes scope of US deal Oil price drop on Trump's 39th 'deal' announcement. Pavlov's dog meets Shcrodinger's cat. investingLive Americas FX news wrap: Another day, another 'deal soon'. This time for sure? US-Iran Schrödinger's cat deal: signed and unsigned at once. Where are we at? Summary Trump cancelled planned Thursday strikes on Iran, citing a near-complete deal with a possible weekend signing in Europe Iran disputed claims of a finalised agreement, with the Supreme Leader's approval reportedly still outstanding Iranian forces blocked a tanker attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz without coordination, with explosions reported near Sirik US forces shot down two Iranian drones near Hormuz after an attempted strike on commercial shipping Oil prices remain subdued, USD ticks higher, regional equities rally led by Japan Asian markets staged a broad rally on Friday even as the situation around the Strait of Hormuz remained fluid, with diplomatic optimism around a potential US-Iran deal offset by continued flare-ups on the water. President Trump cancelled planned US strikes on Iran scheduled for Thursday night, saying a deal had been largely agreed and could be signed as early as this weekend in Europe. However, Iranian officials disputed claims that a final agreement had been reached, with reports suggesting approval from the Supreme Leader remains outstanding. Adding to the uncertainty, Iranian forces blocked a tanker attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz without prior coordination, with the vessel complying after warnings from the IRGC Navy. Explosions were also reported near the coastal town of Sirik in connection with the incident. Separately, US forces shot down two Iranian one-way attack drones near the strait after Iran attempted to strike commercial vessels transiting the waterway. Despite these tensions, oil prices remained subdued, having reversed sharply lower on Thursday after the cancelled strikes removed a chunk of the geopolitical risk premium that had built up over recent sessions. The US dollar saw a modest bounce, clawing back some of Thursday's broad-based weakness. Regional equities, meanwhile, extended Thursday's risk-on tone with solid gains across the board. Japan's Nikkei led the advance, up around 4%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng rose 1.7% and the Shanghai Composite gained 1.6%. The combination of a diplomatically framed de-escalation alongside ongoing military activity around Hormuz leaves markets in something of a holding pattern, pricing in optimism on the deal front while remaining alert to the practical situation on the water, where Iran's actions continue to diverge from the narrative of an imminent, comprehensive resolution. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 路透社调查结果近乎一致：预计澳联储将于6月16日维持利率在4.35%不变 西太平洋银行预计澳联储将在6月15日至16日的会议上维持利率不变，但未来将进一步加息 伊朗革命卫队誓言将作出“令人后悔”的回应，暗示不会在协议问题上妥协 报道 - 美军击落伊朗无人机，该无人机正准备对霍尔木兹海峡航运发动袭击 中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8109（市场预期为6.7640） 韩国央行行长申忠信表示需要加息 日本日经指数因特朗普与伊朗“协议”上涨逾4% 芝加哥商品交易所（CME）将推出微型原油和黄金合约的24/7交易。24/7期货。特朗普反对后，美加推迟戈迪·豪大桥通车。更多相关内容——伊朗封锁霍尔木兹海峡油轮，封锁威胁生效。报道称：新任最高领袖尚未批准特朗普的伊朗协议。受中东冲突影响，新西兰制造业PMI降至49.9。燃料成本与需求构成压力。伊朗因质疑美方协议范围而拦截霍尔木兹海峡油轮。特朗普第39次宣布达成“协议”后油价下跌。巴甫洛夫的狗遇上薛定谔的猫。investingLive美洲外汇新闻综述：又一天，又一个“协议即将达成”。这次是真的吗？美伊“薛定谔的猫”协议：既已签署又未签署。 当前局势如何？简报 特朗普取消了原定于周四对伊朗的空袭计划，称已与伊朗达成近乎完整的协议，可能于本周末在欧洲签署。伊朗方面否认已达成最终协议，据称最高领袖尚未批准该协议。伊朗部队拦截了一艘未经协调试图穿越霍尔木兹海峡的油轮，据报西里克附近发生爆炸。美军在霍尔木兹海峡附近击落了两架伊朗无人机，此前这些无人机曾试图袭击商船。油价维持低迷， 美元小幅走高，日本领涨区域股市周五，尽管霍尔木兹海峡局势依然动荡，亚洲市场仍普遍上涨，围绕美伊潜在协议的外交乐观情绪抵消了海上持续的冲突。特朗普总统取消了原定于周四晚间对伊朗的打击行动，称协议已基本达成，最早可能于本周末在欧洲签署。然而，伊朗官员对已达成最终协议的说法提出异议，有报道称最高领袖尚未批准该协议。 更令人担忧的是，伊朗部队拦截了一艘未经事先协调便试图穿越霍尔木兹海峡的油轮，该船在伊朗革命卫队海军发出警告后遵从了指令。据报道，与该事件相关的爆炸也发生在沿海城镇西里克附近。此外，在伊朗试图袭击穿越该水道的商船后，美军在海峡附近击落了两架伊朗单向攻击无人机。尽管局势紧张，油价仍保持低迷，因周四空袭行动取消消除了近期累积的大部分地缘政治风险溢价，油价周四曾大幅下挫。美元汇率小幅反弹，收复了周四普遍走弱的部分失地。与此同时，区域股市延续了周四的风险偏好情绪，各市场普遍录得可观涨幅。日经指数领涨，涨幅约4%；香港恒生指数上涨1.7%，上证综合指数上涨1.6%。 在外交层面的缓和举措与霍尔木兹海峡周边持续的军事活动交织之下，市场陷入某种观望状态：一方面对协议前景抱有乐观预期，另一方面则密切关注海上的实际情况——伊朗的行动仍与“即将达成全面解决方案”的说法背道而驰。本文作者为investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹。

items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than a couple of low tier releases like the final French and Spanish inflation reports. The ECB yesterday hiked interest rates by 25 bps as widely expected and kept its usual data-dependent and meeting-by meeting approach. ECB sources signalled a pause in July if oil prices do not increase materially but endorsed the market pricing by saying that two more rate hikes are embedded in the projections. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, we have the University of Michigan consumer sentiment report and SpaceX starts trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker $SPCX. There's been a lot of hype around Space X's IPO and given the positive backdrop from Trump's deal optimism, we could see some strong FOMO in the stock market today. Other than that, traders will continue to keep an eye on US-Iran headlines as Iranian media said the Supreme Leader has not approved the deal yet. Looking at all the reports, the prospects of a deal are stronger than ever but there's always some justified skepticism given countless fake Trump's claims. 40th time's the charm? This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 在欧洲时段，除了法国和西班牙的最终通胀报告等几项低关注度数据外，市场日程较为清淡。欧洲央行昨日如市场普遍预期般加息25个基点，并维持其一贯的“数据驱动、逐次会议决定”的政策立场。欧洲央行消息人士暗示，若油价未出现显著上涨，7月将暂停加息；但同时通过表示“预测中已包含两次加息”来认可了市场定价。美股时段 在美股时段，我们将迎来密歇根大学消费者信心指数报告，同时SpaceX将以股票代码$SPCX在纳斯达克开始交易。SpaceX的IPO引发了大量炒作，鉴于特朗普对协议持乐观态度的积极背景，今天股市可能会出现强烈的“害怕错过”（FOMO）情绪。此外，交易员将继续关注美伊局势的最新动态，因伊朗媒体称最高领袖尚未批准该协议。综合各方报道，达成协议的前景比以往任何时候都更加乐观，但鉴于特朗普无数次虚假言论的先例，市场始终存在合理的怀疑态度。 第40次就能成功吗？本文由Giuseppe Dellamotta撰写，发表于investinglive.com。

Other than that, traders will continue to keep an eye on US-Iran headlines as Iranian media said the Supreme Leader has not approved the deal yet. Looking at all the reports, the prospects of a deal are stronger than ever but there's always some justified skepticism given countless fake Trump's claims. 40th time's the charm?
